revision.text:
[[en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu]][[it:Ame-no-Fuyukinu]][[ja:天之冬衣神]][[zh:天之冬衣神]] {{Short description|Japanese deity|qq=}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | children = {{nowrap| [[Ōkuninushi]]{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29|qq=}}
(Ōnamuchi){{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29|qq=}}|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} | consort ={{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}; {{ill|Q38276815|qid=Q38276815|1=Sashikuni Wakahime}}| mother = {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} | cult_centre = [[Hinomisaki Shrine]] | gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | parents ={{ill|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|1=Omizunu}}; {{ill|Futemimi|qid=Q65266228|1=Futemimi}}| offspring = {{ill|Eighty Gods|qid=Q65249011|1=Yasogami}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | member_of = {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qid=Q65249569|1=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities}} }} '''Ame-no-Fuyukinu''' is the son of {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}} and {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, the husband of {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.] and the father of {{nowrap|[[Ōkuninushi]]{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29|qq=}}(Ōnamuchi){{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29|qq=}}|qq=}}. == Lineage and Descendants == The [[Kojiki]] extensively documents his genealogy. It says Amenofuyukinu married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|1=Sashikuni Wakahime|2=ja|3=刺国若比売|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|LT=Sashikuniwaka-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}.{{Cite web |date=2023-11-14 |title=Amenofuyukinu, Amenofukine {{!|qq=}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9436 |access-date=2023-11-14 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231114035349/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9436 |archive-date=2023-11-14 |qq=}} They had a child named [[Ōkuninushi]]{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=121 |title=Ōkuninushi |first1=Kadoya |last1=Atsushi |first2=Yumiyama |last2=Tatsuya |date=20 October 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi).{{Cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=127 |title=Ōnamuchi |first=Kadoya |last=Atsushi |date=21 April 2005 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |access-date=2010-09-29|qq=}} The [[Nihon Shoki]] adds more to the story. It says Susanoo gave him a task. He had to deliver the sword [[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]. This sword was to go to the Plain of High Heaven to be delivered to [[Amaterasu]] == Connection to Hinomisaki Shrine == Amenofuyukinu is worshipped at [[Hinomisaki Shrine]]. Legends say he founded this shrine. The Ono family works as priests there. They claim to be his descendants. The shrine has a ritual. It is called the "Shinken hōten shinji." This means "sword offering ritual." The ritual is based on the Kusanagi sword myth. It remembers the offering of this sword. This sword is one of the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Imperial Regalia of Japan|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}. ==Family tree== {{Eight generations of Izumo|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} [[en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] [[ja:天之冬衣神]] [[zh:天之冬衣神]] {{wikidata link|Q48744862}} [[Category:Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]]
